data.title: Pagaya prices ~$300M auto ABS transaction demonstrating strong investor confidence
data.description:
• Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) has announced the pricing of a new $295M AA-rated auto loan ABS transaction. Amid market volatility, investors continue to seek high-quality assets with attractive yields.
  • Pagaya’s seasoned RPM shelf, now in its sixth year, continues to be a trusted source of consistent execution and performance.
  • “Pagaya’s RPM Shelf has developed a track record of delivering strong returns for its investors. These are the ideal market dislocations where investors can take calculated risk,” said Gal Krubiner, Pagaya’s CEO and Co-Founder.
  • Pagaya’s ability to efficiently execute its latest auto ABS deal, reflects continued strong capital markets demand for Pagaya’s assets.
  • “In an increasingly competitive capital markets environment, our Auto ABS program showcases that demand for and investor confidence in our auto assets remains high,” said Sahil Chandiramani, Pagaya’s Head of Capital Markets.

data.title: Green stock exchange gets go-ahead from SEC
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Stock Exchange approved the Green Impact Exchange's ("GIX") Form 1 application, clearing the way for a stock market focused on the $35T sustainability economy, the company said on Monday.

The new exchange, which expects to start trading in early 2026, will be a listings and trading venue that's focused on sustainability, serving public companies and investors, it said.

GIX’s trading platform will be powered by technology from exchange operator MEMX, and will offer non-tiered, competitive liquidity and quoting programs for members and market makers. GIX will also be part of the National Market System ("NMS"), ensuring best execution for all trades directed or routed to GIX, it added.

"Climate risk is business risk. It’s that simple," said GIX President and co-founder Charles Dolan. "U.S. investors and companies are continuing to pursue sustainability because it makes financial and competitive sense. Public markets like GIX have a pivotal role to play in connecting sustainable investors with companies that understand that."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Evercore gives a rundown on internet stocks ahead of Q1 report
data.description:
Analysts at Evercore think investors are in the midst of a bear market for internet stocks.

They say the average large-cap internet stock is off close to 20% vs. February peak levels and believe over 70% of them are trading within 20% of their 3-year trough valuation levels. In terms of valuation setup, the risk/reward for them is now intrinsically attractive.

The research firm said in the wake of the market selloff, with the exception of Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP), internet stocks have very limited direct exposure to tariff risk, but the cyclical risk to recession is widespread.

Evercore said its March quarter preview work suggests that results should be "reasonably solid" but is increasingly cautious for the June quarter.

Top four large-cap internet longs: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Uber (NYSE:UBER), Meta (NASDAQ:META), and Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL).

Stocks with the most EPS risk for Q1: Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE), Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB), and Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD).

Stocks with the least EPS risk for Q1: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX), Spotify (NYSE:SPOT), and Uber (NYSE:UBER).

data.title: GCM Grosvenor, Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank partner for private markets expansion
data.description:
Alternative asset manager GCM Grosvenor (NASDAQ:GCMG) announced on Monday a strategic partnership with the Tokyo-based Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank to expand private markets offerings.

The collaboration aims to expand SuMi TRUST's initiatives in private markets assets, expand the distribution of GCM Grosvenor's private market investment products to investors in Japan, and jointly develop private markets investment products focused on Japanese markets for distribution to SuMi TRUST clients in Japan as well as to GCM Grosvenor clients globally.

SuMi TRUST is set to acquire $50M of newly issued class A shares of GCM Grosvenor, and committed $100M to Elevate, GCM Grosvenor's private equity seeding strategy.

The two companies target at least an additional $1.5B of AUM from the partnership by 2030.

GCM Grosvenor and Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank have collaborated since 2016 across private equity, credit, and infrastructure strategies.

data.title: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton Q1 sales dip 3% weighing on luxury names
data.description:
Led by declining sales in its Wine & Spirits and Fashion & Leather Goods categories, French luxury giant LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton’s (OTCPK:LVMUY) (OTCPK:LVMHF) total organic sales in the first quarter were down 3%, reflecting a slowdown in consumer spending on high-end merchandise. Wall Street expected sales to improve 1.1%.

The results are weighing on shares of competitors in the luxury space with Kering SA (OTCPK:PPRUY) surrendering early gains to trade 6% in the red, L'Oréal S.A. (OTCPK:LRLCF) (OTCPK:LRLCY) down fractionally, Hermes (OTCPK:HESAY) down 1%, and Prada S.p.A. (OTCPK:PRDSY) down 3%. U.S-listed companies are also down in sympathy with Capri (CPRI), Tapestry (TPR), and Signet (SIG) all lower.

Accounting for nearly half of all sales, revenue in the company’s fashion and leather goods segment fell by 5%. And although champagne sales were slightly lower from a year ago, weak sales of cognac in China and the U.S. resulted in a 9% drop in the wine and spirits category.

By region, European sales rose but were down in the U.S. amid a volatile economic environment. Japanese sales were down as well, while the rest of Asia, including China, saw sales trends similar to those in 2024.

“In a disrupted geopolitical and economic environment, LVMH remains both vigilant and confident at the start of the year. The Group remains focused on the development of its brands, driven by a sustained policy of innovation and investment as well as by a constant quest for quality in its products, their desirability and their selective distribution,” the Paris-based conglomerate said in a statement.

The disappointing performance of its alcohol business fueled rumors that the parent company was considering a sale of its Moët Hennessy business. However, according to CFO Cecile Cabanis, reports of a split between Louis Vuitton and Moët Hennessy are “very wrong.”

Shares of LVMH Moët Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMUY) are more than 6% lower in U.S. trading.

data.title: Dover rated Equal-weight in new coverage at Morgan Stanley on growth doubts
data.description:
Dover (NYSE:DOV) on Monday was rated Equal-weight in new research coverage by analysts at financial-services firm Morgan Stanley. They said the maker of industrial equipment has promising growth catalysts, but they questioned whether higher growth beyond next year is sustainable.

“Dover’s (NYSE:DOV) organic growth should outpace peers in 2025 as it laps acute headwinds, but we are yet to see signs that mid-single-digit organic growth is durable,” Christopher Snyder, research analyst at Morgan Stanley, said in an April 14 report to clients.

Dover has five main operating segments, with corporate brands such as Microwave Products Group, Vehicle Service Group, OK International and Tulsa Winch.

Portfolio repositioning lifts growth outlook

As a diversified global manufacturer, Dover (DOV) historically has delivered modest organic growth — just a 2% compound annual rate over the past decade. But recent strategic moves to “high-grade” the portfolio — emphasizing sectors like biopharma and clean energy — have set the stage for better performance.

Snyder noted that consensus estimates already reflect this expected acceleration, projecting 4% organic growth from 2025 to 2027, nearly triple the company’s long-term average. While Morgan Stanley also models a step-up in 2025, helped by the easing of 300 to 400 basis points of headwinds, the firm remains cautious about what happens once those tailwinds fade.

“If growth ultimately fades back to low single digits on normalizing comps, it will be difficult for the equity to re-rate,” Snyder warned.

Margins, M&A are upside levers

Despite reservations about growth durability, the analysts see the possibility for Dover (DOV) to expand margins and deploy capital effectively. Under CEO Rich Tobin, who took the helm in 2018, the company has shifted from divestitures to acquisitions — armed with $2 billion in cash and generating $1 billion in annual free cash flow.

“As Dover (DOV) shifts from net seller to net buyer, earnings per share and ebitda will grow at a faster rate — potentially acting as a catalyst for upward revisions and multiple expansion,” Snyder said.

Dover (DOV) has also demonstrated solid margin execution, expanding by about 60 basis points annually in the past five years despite mix and volume pressures. With an increasingly lean cost structure and productivity gains, the company could deliver incremental margins above the 25%–35% target shared during its last investor day.

Valuation and rating outlook

Morgan Stanley’s $185 price target is based on a valuation of 18 times next-twelve-month EPS for 2026–2027, a roughly 10% discount to the S&P 500 (SP500) and 20% discount to peers. While that’s slightly tighter than historical levels, it reflects confidence in Dover’s improved portfolio mix and a belief that end markets are near a cyclical trough.

“We think a further re-rating would require more conviction in mid-single-digit organic durability,” Snyder added.

What could change

Bullish scenario: Morgan Stanley would consider turning more positive on Dover (DOV) if signs emerge of sustained strength in higher-margin growth categories — such as thermal connectors, biopharma and carbon dioxide systems — or if the company demonstrates a robust and executable M&A pipeline.

Bearish scenario: Conversely, a more negative outlook could emerge if key growth platforms stall, if pricing power is eroded by Trump 2.0 tariffs, or if Dover’s (DOV) capital deployment underwhelms expectations.

In the meantime, Snyder said Dover (DOV) is well-positioned but fairly valued, with growth expectations high and the burden now on execution.

data.title: Ecopetrol warns of weaker full-year profit, to buy 49% stake in Colombia wind farm from AES
data.description:
Colombian state-run oil firm Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) +0.7% in Monday's trading after warning lower oil prices could reduce its profits this year by as much as 12T pesos (~$2.76B), and saying it also may need to scrap production at some fields and focus on those with lower costs.

"As the price of oil drops, the company will need to monitor that number and make decisions regarding eventual closures of other fields that might not be profitable," President Ricardo Roa said, according to Reuters.

Roa added that 20-30 fields could be at risk of closure; Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) operates 158 fields, although he cautioned that the fields on the chopping block were not major producers.

Each dollar difference on the international market means a 900B peso hit to EBITDA and a 700B peso hit to net profit, Roa added.

Separately, Ecopetrol (EC) said it signed an agreement with AES (AES) to build a cluster of wind farms in La Guajira province, with a capacity of 1,087 MW and a 35 km transmission line; the deal will give it a 49% stake in the project.

Other energy firms have pulled out of wind projects in the province due to opposition from local communities.

data.title: Homeland Security said to probe Wall Street-backed crypto bank Anchorage
data.description:
Anchorage Digital Bank, a crypto startup backed by Goldman Sachs (GS), KKR (KKR) and other Wall Street giants, is being investigated by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, according to a media report dated Monday.

Homeland's El Dorado Task Force, which tackles financial crimes like money laundering, has reached out to former Anchorage Digital staff in recent weeks to learn more about how the company operates internally, Barron's reported, citing people familiar with the inquiry.

Anchorage is mainly recognized for its custody offerings, though it also provides other crypto solutions, such as asset protection and trading tools, to financial institutions and governments.

"The nature of the previously unreported probe was unclear," the article said, noting an Anchorage spokesperson declined to comment, and Homeland representatives did not provide comment.

In 2022, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ordered Anchorage to correct inadequate procedures for customer vetting, tracking suspicious transactions, and anti-money-laundering safeguards, according to a consent order. 

At the time, the regulator highlighted the company's non-compliance with the Bank Secrecy Act, while noting that it was taking steps to resolve the issues raised.

Anchorage has drawn notable Wall Street interest in recent years. In 2021, KKR (KKR) was reportedly planning to back a Series D funding round for Anchorage, valuing it at about $3B. A year later, Apollo Global (APO) picked Anchorage Digital, a federally chartered digital asset bank, to custody a "significant portion" of its digital asset portfolio.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Q1 2025 highlights $3.2T in assets under supervision and $19B in alternative fundraising
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Goldman Sachs (GS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Solomon highlighted strong financial performance in Q1 2025, with net revenues of $15.1 billion and earnings per share of $14.12. Return on equity was 16.9%, and return on tangible equity reached 18%. Solomon emphasized the firm’s resilience and ability to deliver strong results in a dynamic market environment.
  • Global Banking & Markets generated revenues of $10.7 billion, with robust activity in FICC and equities, despite market volatility. Solomon noted that the firm maintained its position as the number one global M&A advisor, citing marquee transactions like Google's $32 billion acquisition of Wiz and the $24 billion take-private of Walgreens Boots Alliance.
  • Asset & Wealth Management achieved record assets under supervision of $3.2 trillion, marking the 29th consecutive quarter of long-term fee-based net inflows. Wealth Management revenue grew 11% year-over-year to $2.2 billion, supported by $1.6 trillion in client assets.
  • CFO Denis Coleman reported a $5.3 billion return to shareholders during the quarter, including $4.4 billion in share repurchases. The firm’s CET1 ratio stood at 14.8%, and total operating expenses were $9.1 billion, with an efficiency ratio of 60.6%.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates ongoing market volatility and uncertainty, with the macroeconomic backdrop showing signs of slowing growth. The firm’s economists project US GDP growth to decelerate from over 2% to 0.5%, raising recession risks.
  • Solomon described elevated client dialogues and a fourth consecutive quarter of backlog growth in investment banking, but cautioned that execution will depend on market conditions. Asset & Wealth Management is expected to sustain high single-digit annual growth in durable revenue streams over time.
  • The firm continues to focus on efficiency initiatives, including leveraging AI to optimize technology and enhance client service. Management reiterated confidence in the long-term benefits of AI adoption for productivity and operational efficiency.

Financial Results

  • Global Banking & Markets produced an ROE of over 20%, with advisory revenues at $792 million. Equity and debt underwriting revenues were $370 million and $752 million, respectively.
  • FICC net revenues were $4.4 billion, with record financing revenues of $1 billion. Equities net revenues reached a record $4.2 billion, driven by strong performance in derivatives and financing activities.
  • Asset & Wealth Management revenues were $3.7 billion, with management and other fees up 10% year-over-year to $2.7 billion. Private banking and lending revenues increased 6% to $725 million.
  • The firm reported $19 billion in alternative fundraising during the quarter, contributing to a total of $342 billion raised since 2019. Gross third-party alternatives fundraising is expected to align with recent years, subject to market conditions.

Q&A

  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore: Asked about deleveraging impacts on financing. CFO Denis Coleman explained that while some balances may decline due to asset price adjustments, client repositioning activity remains significant.
  • Ebrahim Poonawala, Bank of America: Questioned the April market shift and its implications for trading and financing. CEO Solomon acknowledged increased uncertainty but emphasized continued high client activity and strategic repositioning.
  • Betsy Graseck, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the impact of potential regulatory changes on the supplementary leverage ratio (SLR). Solomon highlighted potential benefits to treasury markets and broader financial system efficiency.
  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: Discussed risks to the deal backlog. Solomon noted increased client dialogues and backlog growth but acknowledged the dependency on economic and market conditions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about market activity and regulatory changes but raised concerns about potential slowdowns in client activity and financing. Questions often reflected skepticism about sustainability in the current macroeconomic environment.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing the firm’s strong positioning and adaptability. Solomon frequently highlighted resilience and long-term strategic benefits, while Coleman detailed operational efficiencies and financial discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained steady, with consistent emphasis on execution and client engagement, albeit with increased focus on economic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 net revenues increased from Q4 2024 ($15.1 billion vs. $13.9 billion), alongside higher EPS ($14.12 vs. $11.95) and stronger ROE (16.9% vs. 14.6%).
  • Investment banking backlog continued to grow, maintaining momentum from Q4 2024, though the macroeconomic outlook has become more cautious.
  • Analysts’ focus remained on capital deployment and regulatory impacts, mirroring themes from the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the risk of a slowing global economy and potential recessionary pressures. Regulatory uncertainty and geopolitical tensions were also noted as factors influencing market conditions.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of financing revenues and the potential impact of market volatility on trading and investment banking activities.
  • The firm’s efficiency program and AI adoption were positioned as key strategies to mitigate cost pressures and enhance operational scalability.

Final Takeaway

Goldman Sachs delivered strong Q1 2025 results, driven by record assets under supervision, robust performance in FICC and equities, and continued growth in Wealth Management. While management remains optimistic about the firm’s long-term prospects, they acknowledge near-term economic challenges and market uncertainties. Strategic investments in AI and operational efficiencies are expected to support sustained growth and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: MoneyLion, DailyPay sued by NY Attorney General
data.description:
New York State sued MoneyLion (NYSE:ML) and DailyPay for pushing New Yorkers into short-term expensive payday loans, New York Attorney General Letitia James said on Monday.

The firms took advantage of tens of thousands of New Yorkers with illegal high-interest loans, the AG said. "Both MoneyLion and DailyPay make paycheck advance loans to hourly workers in exchange for fees and tips, pretending to simply be advancing 'earned' wages," the AG's office said in a statement. "Due to the short terms of the loans, the fees MoneyLion and DailyPay charge amount to outrageous annual interest rates in the triple digits, frequently up to 750%."

Furthermore, the office accused both MoneyLion (NYSE:ML) and DailyPay of using "abusive tactics" to push borrowers to take out new loans to cover gaps created by previous loans.

AG James is seeking to end the illegal payday lending practices in New York, obtain restitution for impacted borrowers, and impose civil penalties.

"While many New Yorkers are worried about making ends meet, DailyPay and MoneyLion are making tremendous profits by extracting workers’ hard-earned wages," James said.

In examples the AG's office provided, Daily Pay's most common loan, a seven-day $20 paycheck advance offered for $2.99 reflects an annual interest rate of more than 750%. Meanwhile, more than half of MoneyLion (ML) loans have annual interest rates of more than 500%.

Both MoneyLion (ML) and DailyPay didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

MoneyLion (ML) stock was roughly flat in early afternoon trading.
